text: In this 51-year-old patient, post-procedural arterial bleeding was seen two days after endoscopic mucosal resection for Barrett's esophagus with early carcinoma.
Initially, an attempt is made to apply a hemoclip to achieve hemostasis.
The clip fails to hold due to the rigidity of the wound surface or because the clip is unable to grasp sufficient vital tissue.
The second attempt to apply a Hemoclip also fails.
The clip does not hold on the superficially grasped tissue.
The small vessel stump that is visible is now grasped with bipolar coagulation forceps.
The high frequency generator is previously set to the soft coag mode with effect 4 and a maximum of 80 watts.
Current is applied for approximately 3 to 4 seconds or until a slight coagulation effect can be seen.
